title: 10 best herbal creams for upper abdominal pain

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and bisabolol, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Antispasmodic property: Chamomile's antispasmodic compounds, such as azulene and chamazulene, relax muscle spasms in the stomach and intestines, which can help alleviate cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.
Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects on the digestive system can help reduce irritation and inflammation, promoting relaxation and relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The apigenin and luteolin present in chamomile have been shown to calm the digestive system and reduce spasms, which can contribute to abdominal pain. Additionally, chamomile's ability to relax smooth muscles and reduce inflammation can help alleviate cramps and discomfort in the upper abdominal region, providing relief and promoting a sense of calmness. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which helps alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing muscle contractions and inflammation in the digestive tract.

Luteolin: A flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which helps soothe upper abdominal pain by protecting the digestive tract from oxidative stress and inflammation, and promoting relaxation.

α-Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which helps ease upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and muscle spasms in the digestive tract, and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for upper abdominal pain because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for upper abdominal pain because of their ability to relax the muscles and reduce spasms.
Seeds: They are used to make creams for upper abdominal pain because of their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated and dry them for 2 weeks.
Steep 10g of dried flowers in 100ml of boiling water for 5 minutes to create a floral infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to obtain a clear liquid and discard the solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 10 minutes.
Combine the floral infusion with the cooled wax-oil mixture and whip the blend until it thickens into a creamy texture.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Carminative properties: Peppermint's carminative compounds, like limonene and beta-pinene, help to prevent gas and bloating from forming in the digestive tract, reducing pressure and discomfort that can lead to upper abdominal pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its calming and soothing effects on the digestive system. The menthol content in peppermint creams relaxes the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramping. It also helps to increase the production of digestive enzymes and improve the movement of food through the digestive tract, providing relief from discomfort, bloating, and cramps associated with upper abdominal pain. remedy_constituents: Menthone: Menthone is a monoterpenoid that helps alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and muscle spasms through its analgesic and antispasmodic properties.
Menthol: Menthol is a monoterpenoid that provides relief from upper abdominal pain by stimulating the cold-sensitive nerve endings, which can help reduce spasms and pain perception.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic dimer that helps reduce upper abdominal pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Stems: Used for their essential oils, which contain compounds like menthol and menthone, helping to soothe and calm abdominal discomfort.
Roots: Used for their rhizome, which contains menthol and menthone, providing a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect to alleviate upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves from your garden or local supplier in the morning when dew is still present.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Use a cold press or heat to extract 20 milliliters of essential oil from 100 grams of leaves in 30 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of mentha x piperita essential oil in a double boiler and melt for 10 minutes.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes and pour it into containers to cool and solidify within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with upper abdominal pain because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Steep 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 1l of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to discard solids.
Combine 100g of glycerin with 200g of distilled water in a separate container to create a base.
Mix the base with the strained glycyrrhiza glabra liquid in a ratio of 2:3 to create a final mixture.
Add 2% of a preservative and whip the mixture with a hand mixer until smooth and creamy texture forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Infuse 200g of dried zingiber officinale roots in 1l of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the infused zingiber officinale liquid with 50g of coconut oil and 10g of vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture of oils and waxes into a smooth cream using an electric mixer.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the morning when they are at their highest potency for a minimum of 2 cups.

Soothing and demulcent properties: Dandelion's mucilages create a protective barrier on the digestive tract lining, soothing irritated tissues and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn, indigestion, and stomach ache.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the digestive system.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and bitter compounds, dandelion leaves soothe digestive issues and calm upper abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned dandelion roots in a warm place for at least two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind 20g of dried dandelion roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Mix the ground powder with 100ml of coconut oil and 10ml of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and let it cool before transferring to an airtight jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with upper abdominal pain because they possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that soothe the stomach lining and alleviate discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves for their bioactive compounds responsible for pain relief.
Combine the herbal infusion with a carrier oil and emulsifying wax.
Filter the mixture to remove any solids and achieve a smooth consistency.
Add a preservative and fragrant essential oil to enhance the cream's shelf life and aroma.
Transfer the cream into sterilized jars and store in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain the active compound, chromandrin, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically to the affected area, yarrow cream can help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the abdomen, providing quick relief from cramps and discomfort. Additionally, yarrow's natural antispasmodic properties can help ease spasms and contractions in the digestive tract, alleviating upper abdominal pain associated with conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and menstrual cramps.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds found in yarrow cream, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, help to relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause upper abdominal pain.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm upper abdominal pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, selecting those with no signs of decay or insect damage.
Rinse the harvested plant material in cold water to remove any dirt or debris, then chop it finely.
Steep one part dried achillea millefolium in two parts boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a potent herbal infusion.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids and allowing the liquid to cool slightly.
Combine the cooled herbal infusion with 20% coconut oil and 80% beeswax in a double boiler, heating gently until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that soothe digestive issues. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier weighing about 100 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for at least two weeks or use a food dehydrator on low heat for four hours.
Combine one part dried plantago major leaves with five parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture by storing it in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks shaking the jar daily allowing the plant's active compounds to extract.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl discarding the solids and transfer the herbal cream to sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape creams helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain natural compounds that soothe and relax the muscles in the stomach. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitis vinifera leaves and stems from a trusted source measuring two cups of fresh material.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for six hours or air drying for seven days.
Combine one teaspoon of dried vitis vinifera with one cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for two weeks while occasionally shaking the jar allowing the plant material to steep.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and label the cream for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach area. The natural compounds found in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating cramps and spasms that often accompany upper abdominal pain. Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
Digestive stimulant properties: Thyme's stimulating effects on digestion can help improve gut motility, relieve constipation, and reduce bloating, all of which can alleviate upper abdominal pain.

Barks: Thyme barks contain tannins that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, making it effective for treating upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including thymus vulgaris essential oil, beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in sufficient quantities.
Combine 20ml of thymus vulgaris essential oil with 10g of beeswax and heat gently over low heat for 5 minutes.
Mix 100ml of melted coconut oil with 30g of shea butter in a separate bowl until smooth and well combined.
Gradually add the cooled beeswax-thymus mixture to the coconut-shea butter mixture while whipping continuously for 5 minutes.
Transfer the final cream mixture into sterilized glass containers, allowing it to cool and solidify completely before use within 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for upper abdominal pain are topical ointments infused with natural herbs that help alleviate discomfort and inflammation in the upper abdominal region. They work by reducing pain and inflammation, improving digestive function, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal creams for upper abdominal pain include Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinnamomum verum, Foeniculum vulgare, Aloe barbadensis, Piper nigrum, Capsicum annuum, Zanthoxylum bungeanum, and Cinchona officinalis. If left untreated, upper abdominal pain can lead to fatigue, loss of appetite, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upper abdominal pain that are treated with herbal creams are digestive issues such as cramps, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal creams like peppermint oil cream can soothe stomach upset and relax the muscles in the abdominal area. Other conditions, including menstrual cramps, fibromyalgia, and chronic pain, may also be treated with herbal creams that contain ingredients like arnica, capsaicin, or willow bark extract.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with upper abdominal pain is a blend of Calendula and Peppermint creams. Calendula cream soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Peppermint cream, on the other hand, cools and relaxes the abdominal muscles, easing cramps and spasms. The combination of these two creams works synergistically to alleviate upper abdominal pain and discomfort, making it an effective and natural remedy for various gastrointestinal issues. Apply both creams topically to experience relief.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Drug design, development and therapy, herbal creams can be beneficial for upper abdominal pain due to their potential to protect the mucosa, reduce ulcer effects, and inhibit pro-inflammatory factors. The study reviewed 55 articles on the anti-ulcer activity of medicinal plants and found that natural products rich in active metabolites can manage ulcer disease by improving patients' quality of life. By reducing pain and decreasing lesions, herbal creams may provide an alternative therapeutic approach to conventional treatments for upper abdominal pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root cause of the discomfort. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the digestive muscles, curcumin alleviates cramps and spasms, providing quick relief from upper abdominal pain. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties promote healing and restore balance to the digestive system, allowing the body to recover faster and reducing the likelihood of future episodes. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for those seeking relief.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.
Demethoxycurcumin: It exhibits anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, contributing to the reduction of upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin: This constituent has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Curcuma longa roots are used in creams for upper abdominal pain relief because they contain a significant amount of volatile oils and curcumin, which help reduce inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of curcuma longa rhizomes and clean them thoroughly under running water for 5 minutes.
Boil 500ml of distilled water and add the cleaned curcuma longa rhizomes for 15 minutes.
Combine the boiled mixture with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 200ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes using a wooden spoon.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in a sterilized container for 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compounds in the ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the upper abdominal region. Additionally, ginger has natural relaxant properties, which can help to ease digestive discomfort and promote a sense of calm and well-being. This makes it an effective remedy for managing upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the upper abdominal region.
Shogaols: Shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help block pain signals and reduce inflammation in the upper abdomen.
Bisabolol: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help relax muscles and reduce inflammation in the upper abdominal region, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for making creams, as they contain the highest concentration of active compounds with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of Zingiber officinale are also used in creams due to their high content of gingerols and shogaols, which help in relieving pain and reducing inflammation.
Leaves: Some creams may also use leaves from Zingiber officinale as they contain volatile oils with anti-inflammatory properties that can help in soothing upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and wash them under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Grind the rhizomes into a fine paste using a food processor and set aside for 10 minutes.
Combine the rhizome paste with 8 ounces of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of emulsifying wax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 20 minutes while stirring occasionally with a wooden spatula.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and add 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of jojoba oil for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain compounds like glycyrrhizin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. These creams can also help to relax the muscles in the abdominal wall, reducing spasms and cramps. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory effects of glycyrrhizin may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to upper abdominal pain. remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and soothing the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to pain relief.
Flavonoids: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain, inflammation, and discomfort in the upper abdominal region, promoting overall well-being.
Glycyrrhizin: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent that helps reduce pain and discomfort in the upper abdominal area by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are widely used to make creams for upper abdominal pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are used to make creams for upper abdominal pain due to their ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues.
Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are also used to make creams for upper abdominal pain due to their content of glycyrrhizin, which has antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly under running water for 10 minutes.
Combine 250g of the cleaned glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 1l of distilled water in a large saucepan.
Boil the mixture over medium heat for 30 minutes then reduce heat to low for an additional 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then mix in 100g of beeswax.
Whip 200g of shea butter and 200g of coconut oil separately then combine them with the infused glycyrrhiza glabra liquid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The active compounds in Cinnamomum verum, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, work together to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the abdominal region. Eugenol: Eugenol has a relaxant effect on the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, helping to alleviate spasms and cramps that can cause upper abdominal pain.
Linalool: Linalool has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms in the upper abdomen, thus providing relief from pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for upper abdominal pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Used to make creams for upper abdominal pain due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Rhyzomes: Used to make creams for upper abdominal pain due to their anti-inflammatory and digestive-soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200g of cinnamomum verum bark using 4 liters of 40% ethanol for 2 weeks.
Filter the extracted liquid through a coffee filter to remove any impurities present.
Combine 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 20g of the cinnamomum verum extract to the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture.
Pour the mixture into containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain the active compound anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms in the abdominal region, providing relief from pain. Anethole: Anethole has spasmolytic properties, which helps to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract and relieve upper abdominal cramps and spasms.
Pinene: Pinene exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the upper abdominal region and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relax the muscles.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help to ease cramps and reduce inflammation in the abdominal region.
Stems: The stems are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the upper abdominal area.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500g of dried foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves in a heat-resistant container to infuse oil.
Combine 1 liter of carrier oil with the foeniculum vulgare mixture and heat for 2 hours at 40°c.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the infused oil.
Mix 500g of beeswax with 200g of shea butter and 50g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Combine the infused oil with the beeswax mixture and heat until the mixture reaches 60°c to create the herbal cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The Aloe vera gel contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. When applied topically, the cream can help calm the digestive system and reduce discomfort. remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the upper abdominal region by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Anthraquinones: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to soothe and calm the upper abdominal muscles, reducing pain and discomfort.
Saponins: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the upper abdominal muscles and reduce spasms, thereby relieving pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the stomach lining.
Gel (from Leaves): The clear gel extracted from the leaves is used for its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory effects to provide relief from upper abdominal pain.
Pulp (from Leaves): The pulp of the Aloe barbadensis leaves is used for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of aloe barbadensis gel from the inner part of the leaf with a sharp knife.
Mix the aloe gel with 100g of distilled water and 20g of vegetable glycerin in a clean container.
Add 5g of carbomer and 2g of triethanolamine to the mixture and stir well for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 70c in a double boiler for 30 minutes to facilitate the gelation process.
Pour the mixture into a stainless steel mold and let it set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum creams helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain the active compound piperine, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The piperine in these creams works by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals and reducing inflammation in the affected area. remedy_constituents: Piperine: It helps with upper abdominal pain by inhibiting prostaglandin synthesis, thus reducing inflammation and pain.
Phenolic compounds (e.g., ferulic acid): These compounds help by reducing inflammation, preventing the formation of free radicals, and exerting a protective effect on the stomach lining.
Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol): They help by relaxing the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation, and protecting the stomach lining from damage.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high content of piperine, a compound that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Used due to their bioactive compounds that help in reducing inflammation and relieving pain.
Fruits (Berries): Used due to their potential in reducing nausea and abdominal pain caused by digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the piper nigrum leaves and flowers from mature plants for optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested piper nigrum in a warm dry place for 7-10 days to preserve the herbs.
Grind 50g of the dried piper nigrum leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 50g of the powdered piper nigrum with 100g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 30 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its ability to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The capsaicin present in Capsicum annuum creams stimulates the release of pain-relieving chemicals, such as endorphins and dopamine, which help to alleviate discomfort. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Capsicum annuum creams may help to soothe and calm the muscles in the abdominal region, providing relief from cramps and spasms that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: It helps with upper abdominal pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Ferulic acid: It helps with upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to pain and discomfort in the abdominal region.
Quercetin: It helps with upper abdominal pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing muscle spasms, which can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum are used to make creams for upper abdominal pain because of their high content of capsaicin, a natural pain reliever.

Fruits: The fruits of Capsicum annuum, specifically the peppers, are used to make creams for upper abdominal pain due to their high concentration of capsaicin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation.

Seeds: The seeds of Capsicum annuum are used to make creams for upper abdominal pain because they contain a higher concentration of capsaicin than the leaves or fruits, making them a more potent ingredient for pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 ripe capsicum annuum fruits and gently wash them with distilled water to remove any dirt.
Dry the capsicum annuum fruits in a well-ventilated area for 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 100g of dried capsicum annuum fruits into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of ground capsicum annuum powder with 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the herbal capsicum annuum cream in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the abdominal region. The cream's active compounds, such as furanocoumarins and alkaloids, have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause pain. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can help to ease digestive issues and promote a sense of comfort and relief, making it an effective natural remedy for upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Fragrance Terpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation in the abdominal region, thereby alleviating pain.
Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the upper abdominal region, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the abdominal region, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Barks: Zanthoxylum bungeanum barks are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Seeds: Zanthoxylum bungeanum seeds are used due to their ability to relax the muscles and reduce cramping, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
Stems: Zanthoxylum bungeanum stems are used due to their analgesic properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the upper abdominal region.
remedy_recipe: Extract zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves and stems by steeping 200g of them in 1000ml of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Filter the zanthoxylum bungeanum liquid through cheesecloth to remove the plant material after 30 minutes of steeping.
Combine 300ml of the filtered liquid with 300ml of coconut oil in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the oil is fully incorporated.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar after it has cooled to room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the abdominal region. The natural compounds found in Cinchona officinalis, such as quinine, have been traditionally used to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with various conditions, including digestive issues. By applying the cream topically, the active ingredients can penetrate deep into the skin and muscles, providing rapid relief from upper abdominal pain and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Helps with upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and relieving muscle spasms.
Quinidine: Helps with upper abdominal pain by stabilizing heart rhythm and reducing the severity of cramps.
Cinchonidine: Helps with upper abdominal pain by relaxing smooth muscle and reducing the frequency and severity of contractions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to the presence of quinine, a compound that helps alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
Barks: They are used for their medicinal properties, including pain relief and antimalarial effects, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Stems: They are used due to the presence of alkaloids, such as quinine and quinidine, that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of fresh cinchona officinalis leaves in the early morning after a gentle dew.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 95-105°f for 2-3 hours to remove excess moisture.
Steep 1 ounce of dried cinchona officinalis leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a liquid extract.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil with the extract to create a smooth cream.
